_comment: CNN layer-by-layer configuration per dataset type. See docs/config/cnn_layers_rationale.md for full mathematical justification.

type_b._description: Coloured MNIST numbers on plain backgrounds. Key visual features: digit shape (MNIST strokes), foreground colour (RGB), digit length (1–6 digits), size (large/small). 128x128 RGB input.

cnn_1layer.rationale: Single block captures local edges and colour gradients only. Sufficient for colour classification but limited for full digit recognition. Serves as the simplest baseline in the CNN-depth axis.

cnn_3layer.rationale: Three blocks provide a 7x7 effective receptive field covering full digit strokes and global colour regions. Channel doubling (32→64→128) compensates for spatial resolution loss at each MaxPool. Recommended for Type-B as it balances depth with the simplicity of MNIST-based images.

resnet18_pt.rationale: ResNet18 introduces residual (skip) connections, a qualitatively different inductive bias compared to the sequential CNN1/3Layer models. gradient: dL/dx_l = dL/dx_{l+1} * (dF/dx_l + I) — the identity term I prevents vanishing gradients. ImageNet pretrained weights provide well-conditioned feature initialisation; scratch training on 8,006 images would yield ~1,374 params per sample (high overfitting risk).

type_a._description: Arrangements of coloured geometric shapes with spatial relationships (above/below/left/right). 128x128 RGB. Requires understanding of both shape identity and spatial layout.
type_a.rationale: Spatial relationships (e.g. 'above', 'left of') require a wider effective receptive field to see both objects simultaneously. 3-layer CNN with 7x7 ERF covers enough of the image to capture relative positions.

type_c._description: Tic-tac-toe boards in various game states. 128x128 RGB. 3x3 grid; key features are the presence and position of X/O marks in each of 9 cells.
type_c.rationale: Grid cells are evenly distributed across the image. A 5x5 ERF (2-layer) is sufficient to identify marks within individual cells. 3-layer may introduce unnecessary abstraction for this structured input.
